title: Analytics - Firmware Update Failed
scenario: Verify that after a failed DFU (firmware update), the Firebase dashboard displays default elements plus DFU-specific fields (Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Error Description) and the event counts for related elements increase within a few hours.
steps: Navigate to the Firebase dashboard page.
Verify that default elements are displayed: graph, events in last 30 minutes, event count by country/gender, events per session, app version, app build, app environment.
Verify that additional DFU-specific elements are displayed: Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, and Error Description.
Initiate a DFU (firmware update) that is expected to fail.
Wait a few hours for the event data to propagate.
Check the event count for the DFU-related elements (Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, Error Description) and verify that the counts have increased.
expected: The Firebase dashboard displays all default elements plus Device PID, Charge Unit PID, Update Type, and Error Description. After a failed DFU, the event counts for these DFU-related elements increase within a few hours.
